summary: A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah took effect at 16:00, but Hezbollah violated it, prompting renewed IDF strikes on southern Lebanon. Hezbollah continued attacks, launching 63 rockets, 1 missile, 7 UAVs and other munitions over two days. Iran condemned Israel's actions in Lebanon and discussed financial access under a ceasefire agreement.
key_points: Hezbollah violated the ceasefire that began at 16:00, leading Israel to continue operations as needed (Source: @almogboker78/17523).
The IDF struck Hezbollah infrastructure and operatives in southern Lebanon overnight in response to repeated ceasefire violations (Source: @manniefabian/58626).
Hezbollah carried out 25 attacks between June 17‑18, including 63 rockets, 1 missile, 7 explosive UAVs and 2 IED attacks (Source: @shin_persian/7128).
Iran condemned Israel’s recent aggression against Lebanon and called for Washington’s accountability (Source: @presstv/194791).
Iran’s foreign ministry said the Switzerland meeting is no longer urgent after a digital MoU ending the war was signed (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4138).
Iran will gain staged access to $6 billion of blocked Qatari funds for humanitarian imports during the 60‑day ceasefire extension (Source: @VahidOnline/76508).
IDF spokesperson said Israeli troops have full freedom of action to destroy Hezbollah tunnel systems in the Beaufort Castle area and Ali Taher ridge (Source: @shin_persian/7132).
Israeli air force conducted multiple strikes in southern Lebanon, targeting over 80 Hezbollah positions (Source: @kianmeli1/88077).

sections.executive_summary: A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah began at 16:00, but Hezbollah’s violations have led to renewed Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah continued to launch rockets, missiles and UAVs against Israel, recording 72 munitions over 48 hours. Iran condemned Israel’s actions in Lebanon and discussed financial arrangements tied to the ceasefire.

points: Iran will receive staged access to six billion dollars of Qatari funds blocked by the US for humanitarian imports, released over the 60‑day ceasefire extension (Source: @VahidOnline/76508).
Iran’s foreign ministry stated the Switzerland meeting is no longer urgent after a digital memorandum ending the war was signed by both parties (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4138).
Iran strongly condemned Israel’s latest aggression against Lebanon, urging Washington to be held accountable for war‑mongering (Source: @presstv/194791).
Iran’s national security committee spokesman warned that IAEA inspectors would be denied access to nuclear facilities, contradicting a memorandum of understanding (Source: @yediotnews25/131656).

IDF spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin said Israeli troops have full freedom of action to eliminate threats, continuing operations in the Beaufort Castle area and Ali Taher ridge to destroy major Hezbollah tunnel systems (Source: @shin_persian/7132).
The IDF reported striking Hezbollah infrastructure and operatives in southern Lebanon overnight in response to repeated ceasefire violations (Source: @manniefabian/58626).
Israeli air force conducted strikes on over 80 Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon, reportedly eliminating dozens of militants (Source: @yediotnews25/131658).
IDF spokesperson noted recent detection of two suspicious aerial targets near the Zera'it area, with no casualties reported (Source: @idf_telegram/24397).
Hezbollah violated the ceasefire that started at 16:00, prompting Israeli forces to retain full operational freedom (Source: @almogboker78/17523).
Hezbollah launched 25 attacks between June 17‑18, including 63 rockets, 1 missile, 7 explosive UAVs, 2 IEDs and a terrorist infiltration attempt (Source: @shin_persian/7128).
A video showed a Hezbollah‑operated drone‑filled explosive targeting an Israeli Merkava tank near the town of Yehmar al‑Shaqif in southern Lebanon (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/371677).
Hezbollah reportedly used a drone‑laden explosive vehicle that killed four fighters after a missile strike (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/371680).
Israeli forces reported the death of Lt. Col. Dor Gadlia, commander of the 52nd Battalion, in combat in southern Lebanon (Source: @yediotnews25/131651).
Sheikh Naeem Qasim claimed Iran is pressuring Syria to intervene from the east, aiming to block weapon flows and impose a comprehensive financial siege (Source: @naya_foriraq/79313).

key_developments: Hezbollah violated the 16:00 ceasefire, leading Israel to maintain unrestricted operational freedom in southern Lebanon (Source: @almogboker78/17523).
IDF conducted overnight strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in response to repeated ceasefire breaches (Source: @manniefabian/58626).
Hezbollah executed 25 attacks over two days, firing 63 rockets, 1 missile, and deploying UAVs and IEDs (Source: @shin_persian/7128).
Iran condemned Israel’s latest aggression in Lebanon and highlighted US responsibility (Source: @presstv/194791).
Iran secured staged access to $6 billion of Qatari funds for humanitarian imports during the ceasefire (Source: @VahidOnline/76508).
Israeli air force targeted over 80 Hezbollah positions in southern Lebanon, eliminating dozens of militants (Source: @kianmeli1/88077).
Hezbollah used a drone‑laden explosive vehicle that killed four of its fighters after a missile strike (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/371680).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to test the ceasefire by launching rockets, missiles and UAV attacks, indicating a sustained low‑intensity threat to Israel. Israeli forces retain full operational freedom, focusing on destroying tunnel networks and command centers in southern Lebanon, which may provoke further escalations. Iran’s diplomatic moves, including financial access and condemnation of Israeli actions, suggest a broader regional dimension that could influence future hostilities.
sections.regional_response: Iran’s condemnation of Israeli strikes and its negotiation of humanitarian fund releases reflect an attempt to leverage diplomatic pressure while maintaining support for allied groups. The staged release of Qatari funds under the ceasefire may bolster humanitarian efforts but also serves as a political tool for Iran. International actors, notably the United States and Switzerland, remain engaged in negotiations, though the urgency of the MoU appears to have diminished.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah’s drone and missile capabilities is critical, as recent attacks demonstrate evolving tactics. Israeli freedom of action in the Beaufort Castle and Ali Taher ridge areas suggests a focus on degrading Hezbollah’s command infrastructure. Iranian financial maneuvers indicate potential for increased resource flow to proxy groups if ceasefire conditions evolve.
